column1: Magical horses and similar animals have been imagined in various cultures for much of history
column2: but the unicorn as we know came from Ancient Greece. Greek writers like Ctesias record horses with horns and wings living to the east
column3: likely misinterpereting stories about real animals like rhinos and bulls. Later
column4: Unicorns became a key part of folklore in medieval Europe
column5: and even became the national animal of Scotland. Today
column29: A unicorn is a fictional creature. It looks like a horse with a long spiral horn on it's forehead, like a narwhal. Unicorns are often drawn with rainbow colored manes and white coats of fur, but they can come in many different colors. Unicorns are said to have various magical powers, and are also said to be fast and hard to catch. Often, when something is extremely rare but very useful, it will be compared to a Unicorn. Many stories include unicorns as characters, from ancient folklore to modern shows and movies.
